description: Phase WX-D (2026-05-19) — hot-patchable rules for CLI failure classification. Read by CLIFailureMitigator (skeleton; not yet wired into AIHarness) after each CLI Outcome whose terminationReason indicates failure. Rules are checked top-to-bottom by priority (higher first); first match wins. Vendor error-shape changes ship as PRs to this file; no Swift rebuild required (CLAUDE.md ABSOLUTE RULE #1).

schema.rules[].id: Stable identifier used in telemetry + chat status messages.
schema.rules[].description: Human-readable explanation of when this rule fires and why the chosen action is right.
schema.rules[].priority: Integer. Higher priority rules are checked first. Tie-break: first in array.
schema.rules[].match.backend: Optional. 'claude' | 'codex' | 'gemini' | '*'. Restricts which CLI backend this rule applies to.
schema.rules[].match.terminationReasonContains: Optional substring match against Outcome.terminationReason. Skips the event scan when absent (rule fires on any failed outcome).
schema.rules[].match.eventType: Optional. Event type string (e.g. 'rate_limit_event', 'error', 'tool_error'). When set, at least one event of this type must exist in the transcript.
schema.rules[].match.bodyContains: Optional substring match against the raw JSON line of the matched event.
schema.rules[].match.jsonEquals: Optional dict of dotted-path -> expected string. Every path must resolve and equal its expected value within the matched event's JSON body.
schema.rules[].params: Action-specific knobs. See per-action notes below.
schema.rules[].userChatTemplate: Status message appended to the wizard session chat. Supports {placeholder} substitution from the matched event's JSON body.

cascade.description: Skip the current backend and move directly to the next in the cascade order. No retry on the same backend.

retryWithParamTweak.description: Re-run the SAME backend with adjusted runtime parameters. Use when the failure is transient and a config tweak (backoff, raised maxTurns, raised timeout) is likely to succeed.

params.backoffSeconds: Integer literal OR a path expression like 'header:retry-after' or 'event:rate_limit_info.resetsAt' (resolved to delta from now). Default 0 (no delay).
params.maxRetries: Integer. Cap on retry attempts. Default 1.
params.maxTurnsBump: Integer added to runtime maxTurns. Default 0.
params.timeoutBumpSeconds: Integer added to runtime timeout. Default 0.

retryWithContentPatch.description: Append/override CLAUDE.md (or AGENTS.md / GEMINI.md) context for this backend in the workDir, then re-run the SAME backend. Use when the failure is a CLI-specific behavior (e.g. context overflow, tool misuse) the agent context can correct.

params.agentContextAppend: Text to append to <backend>'s agent-context markdown file in workDir. Plain text; no templating.

rules.description: Claude CLI emitted a rate_limit_event indicating the 5-hour subscription window is exhausted AND org-level overage is disabled. Pay-as-you-go overflow won't fire, so retrying the same backend with backoff is pointless — the only path forward is to cascade to codex/gemini.
rules.userChatTemplate: Claude CLI: 5-hour rate-limit window exhausted, overage disabled at org level. Cascading to next backend.

_realDataNotes: 2026-05-19 — captured first real rate_limit_event body (74f5bebb session, Phase WX-D inline archive). status='allowed' (warning, not exceeded), overageStatus='rejected', resetsAt=1779253200 (~1hr ahead). The rule above strictly matches status='exceeded' — a status='allowed' warning is informational only and should not trigger cascade.

Need more failure shapes before adding rules for: codex maxTurns-exhausted-but-progress (current data shows pure exploration loops), gemini tool_error variants, auth-expired patterns. Add a rule here only when we have a real captured event body that justifies the action.
